clinical_history: This ECG is from an elderly woman and is most consistent with which one of the following severe abnormalities?
diagnosis: This ECG shows an extremely wide complex rhythm (QRS duration 0.28 second) at a rate of 70 bpm. This pattern is approaching that of a so-called sine-wave morphology indicative of severe hyperkalemia. Atrial activity is not apparent. The patients serum potassium level done about ½ hr before was 6.8 mEq/L (and was likely higher at the time of this ECG).  She had end-stage renal disease with a BUN of 78 mg/dL and a serum creatinine of 5.2 mg/dL. Emergency therapy of hyperkalemia is required based on this ECG finding.
